steps.title: Look for inflated self-regard
steps.content: The claim is that sophists have an exaggerated sense of their own wisdom. The quotation should show a sophist presenting his own ideas as clever and expecting a customer to grasp them immediately.

steps.title: Separate description from self-display
steps.content: A shows dismissal of a customer, not self-praise. B describes what sophists teach, and C is encouragement from another character. The quotation about 'clever dogma' most directly displays inflated confidence in the sophist's own wisdom. The answer is D.
